title: OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface
content: The SSD1306 drives a 128×64 (or 128×32) pixel monochrome OLED panel via I²C (address 0x3C or 0x3D) or SPI. No backlight required; each pixel emits light independently, yielding high contrast (>2000:1) and wide viewing angle (>160°). Supply: 3.3–5 V; typical panel current 20 mA at full brightness.
extended_content: Initialisation sequence: write 21 configuration bytes to enable charge pump, set display clock, multiplex ratio, COM pin configuration, and turn on display. The entire 128×64 framebuffer = 1024 bytes sent over I²C using page addressing or horizontal addressing mode. Adafruit SSD1306 + GFX libraries provide text, shape, and bitmap drawing. Pixel coordinate origin: top-left (0,0). Partial updates (single-page refresh) reduce flicker for animations. Operating temperature: −40 °C to +85 °C. OLED lifespan: ~50 000–100 000 hours at half-brightness; screen-burn in if static content for >1000 h. Common module: 0.96" 128×64 I²C OLED (£1–2), widely available.
source: Solomon Systech SSD1306 Advance Information Rev 1.1; Adafruit SSD1306 Library Documentation
